title: cuphea corisperma subsp. hexasperma
intro: Cuphea corisperma subsp. hexasperma, also known as Mexican heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and rounded, green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Mexican heather include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve mood, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Cuphea corisperma subsp. hexasperma can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
